entry.title: CAD Interfaces Now Available in SAP Integrated Product Development
entry.content:
Welcome to this blog post! Effective June 15th, 2026, SAP Integrated Product Development now supports direct integration with leading MCAD authoring tools. This marks a significant milestone for product-centric industries, closing a critical gap between engineering design environments and enterprise product data management.

Stronger Product Data. Stronger Supply Chain.

With these new integrations, SAP Integrated Product Development takes a major step forward for product-centric industries. Customers can now:

  • Manage and govern product data in a single system of record
  • Directly connect MCAD authoring tools to SAP Cloud ERP, without manual re-entry or version mismatches
  • Seamlessly transition from EBOM (Engineering Bill of Materials) to MBOM (Manufacturing Bill of Materials)
  • Improve cross-functional collaboration across engineering, sourcing, and manufacturing

These capabilities strengthen the digital thread across product development and supply chain operations, helping companies accelerate innovation and improve time-to-market.

 

What does the overall architecture for CAD integration with SAP IPD look like?

 

 

Which CAD Systems Are Now Supported?

SAP Integrated Product Development now provides dedicated plug-in adapters for three major MCAD platforms, with a fourth on the way:

  • Autodesk Inventor
  • SOLIDWORKS
  • Solid Edge
  • AutoCAD (coming soon)

These CAD plug-ins directly integrate CAD structures with design items and product structures in SAP Integrated Product Development, enabling seamless data synchronization and improving overall data quality across the process.

A Closer Look at the CAD Adapter Capabilities

Each CAD adapter is delivered as a plug-in that installs within the respective CAD environment, allowing engineers to work in their preferred tools while maintaining a direct connection with SAP.  The Product Data Integration SaaS application serves as the backbone for this connectivity, integrating data from external, non-SAP authoring systems as the authoritative source of product data.

Key capabilities include:

  • Upload: Import metadata, structures, and files from external CAD authoring systems into SAP Integrated Product Development
  • Retrieve: Read metadata and product structures from SAP and download the corresponding files back to the CAD environment
  • Design item versioning: Each save action from the external CAD system creates a new design item minor version, providing full traceability
  • Status lifecycle management: Define status profiles, assign them to design item types, and manage lifecycle transitions
  • Search and visibility: Find design items using filters such as Design Number, Design Type, Status, and Version
  • Collaboration integration: Assign design item versions to collaborations for cross-functional review and approval
  • Product structure derivation: Derive product structures from uploaded CAD data for use in the Enterprise Product Structure application
  • Visualization support: Derive 3D visualization from native 3D CAD authoring files associated to product structure.
  • Configure design types: Create and maintain design types to differentiate integrations to external authoring systems and control behavior.

For full technical details, refer to the Product Data Integration Feature Scope Description.

 

Documentation and Download Links

SAP Help Portal - Admin and User Guides for each adapter:

  • Autodesk Inventor: Admin Guide | User Guide
  • SOLIDWORKS: Admin Guide | User Guide
  • Solid Edge: Admin Guide | User Guide

Software Download Center - Download plug-ins from the SAP Software Download Center (search for "PDI" or click on the links below):

  • Autodesk Inventor plug-in: Download
  • SOLIDWORKS plug-in: Download
  • Solid Edge plug-in: Download

 

Pricing and Supplement

The CAD integrations are delivered as capabilities within SAP Integrated Product Development, with each integration representing a distinct capability.  They follow a capacity unit–based consumption model:

  • Each CAD integration capability consumes a defined number of capacity units
  • Users only consume capacity units based on the specific capabilities they actively use

This model allows organizations to scale usage based on actual needs while maintaining clear visibility into consumption.

For detailed information on capacity unit allocation per capability, please refer to the product supplement.

Conclusion

The availability of CAD adapters for SAP Integrated Product Development marks a key milestone in connecting engineering design with enterprise operations. By enabling direct, bidirectional integration between leading MCAD tools and SAP Cloud ERP, organizations can manage product data within a single system of truth - reducing errors, accelerating time-to-market, and strengthening the digital thread from concept to delivery.

Key benefits include:

  • Integrate CAD tools directly with SAP Cloud ERP, eliminating data silos between engineering and operations
  • Enable end-to-end supply chain transformation starting from the design phase
  • Enhance user experience by allowing CAD users to work within their familiar tools while staying connected to SAP

Stay tuned for the release of the AutoCAD integration, and for further updates as the CAD adapter portfolio continues to expand.

 

Screenshots of the current CAD adapters

Autodesk Inventor

 

 

Solidworks

Coming Soon….

Solid Edge

Coming Soon….

More Information

Don't hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up to date with our latest enablement assets: https://www.linkedin.com/in/annette-garcia-lorenzo

entry.title: Re: WBS Sales Contract Position - WBS Element
entry.content:
Guten Tag @Bergj 

Vielen Dank für Ihre Frage.

Die Kontierung eines Verkaufskontrakts auf ein PSP Element wird im Standard nicht unterstützt, weder als fachlich nutzbare Kontierung auf Kopfebene noch auf Positionsebene. Dieses Verhalten ist ausdrücklich in KBA 2668268 festgehalten. Die Meldung V2509 kann erscheinen, wenn man eine Zuordnung versucht.

Das Feld PSP Element ist zwar im Migrationstemplate bzw. In der technischen Struktur der Verkaufskontraktposition vorhanden. Die API Dokumentation listet beispielsweise das Feld WBSElement in der Entität Sales Contract Item. Weil diese Technischen Feld vorhanden ist, bedeutet nicht, dass die Kontierung für jeden Verkaufsbelegtyp fachlich freigegeben ist. Die Anwendungsprüfung für Verkaufskontrakte bleibt bestehen.

Daher lässt sich das Feld auch nicht über UI Anpassung als unterstützte Kontierung im Vertrag aktivieren.

Ich empfehle folgender Prozess:

  1. Legen Sie den Verkaufskontrakt ohne PSP Element an.
  2. Erstellen Sie den späteren Kundenauftrag mit Bezug zum Verkaufskontrakt.
  3. Ordnen Sie das abrechnungsfähige PSP Element auf Positionsebene des Kundenauftrags zu.

Die Zulässigen Positionstypen und Voraussetzungen sind unter folgender Link beschrieben.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/3804ed4c209a408d8eb660a67172139a.html?locale=de-DE

Das PSP Element muss dabei als Abrechnungselement gekennzeichnet und für die verwendete Kundenauftragsposition unterstützt sein. Die Zuordnung kann auch bei Kundenaufträgen erfolgen, die mit Referenz auf einen Vorgängerbeleg Erstellt wurden.

Die genannte Funktion bei Serviceverträgen ist ein anderer fachlicher Prozess. In Serviceverträgen können PSP Elemente als Merkmale des Ergebinsobjektes zugordnet werden, wie in Serviceverträge verwalten beschrieben. Diese Funktion is nicht auf Verkaufskontrakte übertragbar.

Ein kundeneigenes Feld würde lediglich eine Referenz speichern, aber keine echte CO Kontierung oder Standardintegration erzeugen.

 

Lassen Sie mich Wissen, wenn Sie weitere Fragen haben.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: 🐍 July Developer Challenge Week 1 — Model the Board
entry.content:
namespace snakeladder;

type TurnStatus : String enum {
  Waiting;
  Playing;
  Moving;
  Blocked;
  Finished;
}

entity BoardSquares {
  key square       : Integer;
      snakeTo      : Integer;
      ladderTo     : Integer;
      isDoubleHead : Boolean default false;
}

entity GameSessions {
  key ID         : UUID;
      name       : String(100);
      createdAt  : Timestamp;
      startedAt  : Timestamp;
      finishedAt : Timestamp;
      winner     : Association to Players;
}

@flow.status: TurnStatus
entity Players {
  key ID         : UUID;
      session    : Association to GameSessions;
      name       : String(100);
      @readonly
      position   : Integer default 1;
      @readonly
      TurnStatus : TurnStatus default #Waiting;
      turnOrder  : Integer;
      lastRoll   : Integer;
}
using { snakeladder } from '../db/schema';

service GameService {
  entity GameSessions as projection on snakeladder.GameSessions;

  @flow.status: TurnStatus
  entity Players as projection on snakeladder.Players actions {
    @from: [ #Waiting ]
    @TO: #Playing
    action startTurn();

    @from: [ #Playing ]
    @TO: #Moving
    action rollDice() returns Integer;

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Waiting
    action confirmMove();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Blocked
    action blockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Blocked ]
    @TO: $flow.previous
    action unblockPlayer();

    @from: [ #Moving ]
    @TO: #Finished
    action winGame();
  }

  entity BoardSquares as projection on snakeladder.BoardSquares;
}


// I kept the suggested TurnStatus enum values and did not add any new ones, because Waiting, Playing, Moving, Blocked, and Finished already cover the player lifecycle

entry.title: Re: Mass Upload option for the Physical Inventory Count
entry.content:
Dear @haja_peermohammedh7a1ec0,

Yes. For mass physical inventory count SAP provides released standard options

For BAPI-based integration the relevant Public Cloud communication scenario is SAP_COM_0107 – Physical Inventory Document Integration. SAP lists the following released BAPIs for this scenario:

  • BAPI_MATPHYSINV_COUNT – Material Physical Inventory – Enter Count
  • BAPI_MATPHYSINV_CHANGECOUNT – Material Physical Inventory – Change Count
  • BAPI_MATPHYSINV_CREATE_MULT – Material Physical Inventory – Create

More details below:

SAP Help – BAPIs/IDocs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP Community Calling BAPIs in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Please note that for new third-party integrations, if the released OData API covers the required process the cleaner Public Cloud approach is to use the released OData API. The BAPI option remains available where it is explicitly released by SAP for the relevant communication scenario, such as SAP_COM_0107 for physical inventory document integration

If the requirement is a business-user Excel upload of physical inventory count results, the standard app is Manage Physical Inventory Count – F5430. SAP Help confirms that this app supports downloading and uploading Excel files for entering count results and that count results can be uploaded back in the Manage Physical Inventory Count app

More details below:

SAP Help – Manage Physical Inventory Count
SAP Help – Physical Inventory Process

SAP also describes the physical inventory count entry options, including barcode scanning, Excel download/upload and manual entry

More details below:

SAP Help – Preparation and Entry of Required Stock Data

As an alternative to BAPIs, SAP also provides the released OData API API_PHYSICAL_INVENTORY_DOC_SRV / Physical Inventory Document API. For count results the count can be stored or changed using PATCH on the A_PhysInventoryDocItem entity

More details below:

SAP Help – Physical Inventory Document API: Count Physical Inventory Document Items

Summary:

Use Manage Physical Inventory Count F5430 for Excel-based mass count upload. Use SAP_COM_0107 with BAPI_MATPHYSINV_COUNT / BAPI_MATPHYSINV_CHANGECOUNT for BAPI-based integration. For OData-based integration use API_PHYSICAL_INVENTORY_DOC_SRV

Best Regards,

Dawid

1. Following SAP products are supported for connection with Cloud ALM, including S/4HANA and Business Suite 7 or NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP:

entry.title: Re: We need your guidance regarding Cost Center Budgeting
entry.content:
Good day @srimal_athukoralae797cf 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, you can achieve a separate budget control but the important point is that Cost Center Budget Availability Control performs the check at Cost Center plus G/L Account Group level.

Therefore, when several G/L accounts belong to the same account group, their budgets are pooled. A posting may exceed the amount uploaded for one G/L account without triggering an error, provided the total budget of that account group and cost center is still available. See KBA 3505874 for more details.

So, in simple terms:

  • Uploading separate budget lines by G/L account does not automatically create separate AVC limits.
  • The account group assigned in the AVC profile determines which accounts share a budget.
  • To control each G/L account separately, create a separate account group containing only that specific G/L account.

Here is a quick guide:

Create separate account groups in Manage Global Hierarchies and select type G/L Account Hierarchy.

Create separate nodes, for example:

  • Z_TRAVEL containing only G/L account 610000
  • Z_TRAINING containing only G/L account 620000
  • Z_CONSULTING containing only G/L account 630000

Keep all account groups used by one AVC profile under the same hierarchy. See KBA 3504317.

 

Maintain the AVC profile in the configuration activity Maintain Budget Availability Control Profile for Cost Centers. Open the Profile assigned to the affected cost center, Assign the separate account groups, maintain All Activity Group (++), and set the required tolerance, for example 100% with message type Error when postings must be blocked after the full budget is consumed.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bd39d476d1e34b48afed98759286efd6/b588cc2d87254bcd983d3d927559d9cb.html?locale=en-US

3519228 - Error FCO_AVC_CC013 while activating budget availability control for cost centers - SAP for Me

Then you need to activate the profile on the cost center. In Manage Cost Centers (new version),  select the cost center and choose Edit.

Make sure you verify the following:

  • Budget Availability Control Profile is assigned
  • Availability Control is On
  • Lock Commitment Updates is Off when PR and PO commitments must consume budget. See KBA 3505874 for more insights.

 

Then use Import Financial Plan Data to test the result by uploading the separate budget amounts.  

Thereafter, verify them in Cost Center Budget Report. 

Post an amount that exceeds the budget for one G/L account while another account still has available budget. The posting should blocked for exceeded G/L account because it belongs to its own account group. The unused budget of another account group is not considered.

If the requirement also includes PR and PO commitments, you must activate scope item 213 Predictive Commitments Management. See KBA 3505874 for more details.

To summarise:

  • Separate control is possible through the account-group design.
  • Create one account group per G/L account, assign those groups to AVC profile 102781, activate the profile on the cost center and test with separate budget values.
  • Accounts within the same account group will continue to share the available budget.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: EWM: Split 1 wave item for direct picking and 2 step...
entry.content:
• If you want a purely standard solution, you’ll need to adjust ERP delivery creation so that PAL and CAR are separate items.

  • If you want to keep ERP untouched, then a custom BAdI implementation in EWM for WT creation is the pragmatic way forward. You can define rules like:

    • If quantity ≥ 1 PAL → create WT for PAL direct to stage.

    • Remaining CAR → merge with other CAR wave items for two-step.

entry.title: Default Counted Date Based on Physical Inventory Count Entry Date
entry.content:
We are using the Physical Inventory process with Book Inventory Freeze enabled.

Current process:

  • Users may complete the physical count several days later, for example on 30.06.2026.
  • During count entry, users need to manually change the Counted Date / Count Date of Uncounted Items from current date (30.06.2026) to planned count date (27.06.2026)
Does any standard configuration or custom extensibility option to default the Counted Date automatically instead of requiring users to update it manually?

entry.title: Re: #CoE#TFAL How to Handle Mass Upload of Transactions f...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please check following workaround approaches,

  1. API-Based Upload via Supplier Invoice or Business Partner APIs The standard Supplier Invoice API (Create, Read, Release, Reverse) does not include address information fields, which are mandatory for one-time vendor invoices. As a result, this API cannot be used directly for one-time vendor invoice creation. A custom integration layer (e.g., on SAP BTP) would be needed to enrich the payload with address data before posting. For Business Partner mass creation, the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER OData API (communication scenario SAP_COM_0008) supports create, read, update, and delete operations for Business Partner, Customer, and Supplier objects. 

  2. SAP BTP Side-by-Side Extension with Custom Business Rules A side-by-side extension on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) can be used to build a custom upload app that applies business rules for data mapping and transformation before delivering posting records to S/4HANA via SAP Standard APIs. This approach preserves upgrade stability of S/4HANA while enabling tailor-made processing logic for one-time vendor scenarios. A manual upload capability can be provided first, with automated delivery added in subsequent iterations. 

  3. SAP Build Process Automation / RPA SAP Build Process Automation can be used to orchestrate the mass processing workflow and call the released Business Partner or invoice APIs. This should be treated as a governed automation layer rather than a standalone upload app. For purchase documents, RPA integration with S/4HANA (scope items 48M/6VW) is also a recognized approach for bulk creation. 

  4. "Create Supplier Invoice – Advanced" (MIRO) as a Manual Fallback For lower volumes or error correction scenarios, the "Create Supplier Invoice – Advanced" (MIRO) app is the recommended SAP workaround for posting invoices for one-time vendors when standard import apps are not supported.

Best regards,

Yong

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 1909 to SAP S/4HANA 2025 Upgrade Preparat...
entry.content:
As a PP/QM consultant preparing for an upgrade from SAP S/4HANA 1909 to 2025, your main responsibilities are to understand simplification changes, validate business processes, and ensure readiness for new innovations. You should focus on SAP Readiness Check, simplification item analysis, Fiori adoption, and regression testing of PP/QM processes.

Key Preparations Before the Upgrade

1. Understand Simplification Items
Review the Simplification Item Catalog for S/4HANA 2025. Identify changes relevant to PP (Production Planning) and QM (Quality Management):

  • PP: Changes in MRP, production versions, advanced ATP, embedded PP/DS.
  • QM: Integration with Fiori apps, inspection lot handling, defect management.

Document which existing transactions or processes will be impacted.

2. Run SAP Readiness Check
Perform the SAP Readiness Check to assess Custom code usage and compatibility, Business process relevance and Add-ons and industry solutions. This gives you a clear list of potential issues before the upgrade.

3. Custom Code & Enhancements
Work with ABAP team to analyze Z-programs, user exits, BADIs. Identify obsolete or incompatible code. Plan remediation or redesign for PP/QM-specific enhancements.

4. Data & Master Data Validation
Ensure production versions are correctly maintained (mandatory in newer releases). Validate inspection plans, characteristics, and master data in QM. Clean up obsolete or inconsistent data to avoid migration errors.

Activities During the Upgrade Project

Functional Consultant Responsibilities

Fit-to-Standard Workshops:
Compare current processes with S/4HANA 2025 standard capabilities.

Testing:
Regression testing of PP/QM processes (order creation, confirmations, inspection lots). End-to-end integration testing with MM, SD, and WM/EWM.

Fiori Enablement:
Learn and test new Fiori apps for PP/QM. Train users on role-based access and new UX.

Authorizations:
Validate PFCG roles and adjust for new apps and transactions.

Cutover Planning:
Support data migration validation. Ensure inspection lots, production orders, and quality results are migrated correctly.

entry.title: Overlapping messages during quantity conf. result in incorrect order statuses (pcnf, final cnf)
entry.content:
Hi,

Situation Description

Quantity confirmations are sent from DM to the ERP every 5 minutes.

The last confirmations get a final confirmation flag.

During the production period, the order in the ERP may be locked, causing the message in the Integration Message to fail.

 

Behavior in DM

The retry settings in the Integration Message Dashboard allow the confirmation messages to be retried multiple times.

 

Problem

Quantity confirmations that set the status to “partially confirmed” or “final confirmation” must be processed in the correct sequence.

If an order is locked in the ERP, a “Final Confirmation” may be reset in the ERP by a later “Partially Confirmed” entry (done by the retry from DM).

If no new “Final Confirmation” is then sent from the DM, the status in the ERP remains “Partially Confirmed,” which is incorrect.

 

Question

What are appropriate approaches for such dependent bookings?

 

My Approaches

Solution 1 – Workaround

Using a job in DM that periodically reads all SFC Done entries.

I simply submit a confirmation with a quantity of 0 with a final confirmation.

This ensures that the status is ultimately correct in the ERP.

Solution 2 - Asyn CPI

According to the documentation, it is possible to configure asynchronous commit for certain collaboration links/directives.

There are also sequential queues that appear to be designed specifically for this purpose.

However, asynchronous posting requires at least the “Standard” plan in the CPI. The DM, however, only comes with a “Basic_edition.”

How “intelligent” is the sequential asynchronous queue? Does the logic here differentiate by plant, order, or other IDs?
If everything goes into a single queue and a message is sent to the ERP due to an error, does that block all messages, even those that might come from various plants?

Solution 3 – Build Your Own Queuing Logic

This approach is more theoretical and, in my opinion, not practical.


Do you have any suggestion how this can be solved?
We are running in this problem at several Customers and I assume most of the Customers have the problem.

Thank you very much in advance for any suggestions.

Max

SAP Digital Manufacturing

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Dataaction resultlookup
entry.content:
Hey @MK70 ,
this is how SAC's scope narrowing works inside conditionals, and it trips up a lot of people at first.


What's happening it that when you evaluate IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Dim1]="a", [d/Dim2]="b") != 0, that condition narrows the active scope for the entire THEN block to just Dim1=a, Dim2=b. Any RESULTLOOKUP you call inside that block inherits that narrowed scope unless it explicitly overrides those same dimensions. So even though your MEMBERSET said Dim3={g,h,i}, the second RESULTLOOKUP([d/Dim3]="g") still sees Dim1=a, Dim2=b in effect from the condition, it doesn't revert to the original MEMBERSET scope.


Reordering fixes it because if you move the second RESULTLOOKUP before the IF, it runs in the original unconstrained scope (the full MEMBERSET range), so it doesn't get narrowed by the condition.


A workaround that you could try is to either pull independent RESULTLOOKUP calls outside and before the IF block (cleanest), or explicitly restate your MEMBERSET scope inside the THEN block if you need to override the condition's narrowing.


It's documented behavior, just not super intuitive. Once you know it's there, it's easy to work around. You can read it here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/766b9da1890d431ca29927daee4811b4.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

entry.title: Re: Error on calling a CAP Service/OData from SAP MDK
entry.content:
Hi Govardan,

The concept of overriding Actions in JavaScript Rules (described here: https://help.sap.com/doc/f53c64b93e5140918d676b927a3cd65b/Cloud/en-US/docs-en/guides/getting-started/mdk/development/executing-actions.html#overriding-an-action-in-javascript-rule) is the best option to pass dynamic parameters to the MDK Action. 

The first issue which I observed in your JS Code is the structure of how you are passing the parameters to your MDK CallFunction Action. You need to make sure, that the Properties {} structure follows the same structure from the CallFunction Action. In your case, it should look like the following: 

{
   "Name": "/*******SyncApp/Actions/Docket/CreateDeliveryNote_G/CreateDeliveryNote_Online.action",
   "Properties": {
      "Target": {
         "Function": {
            "Parameters": {
               "contractNo": payload.contractNo,
               "custPoNumber": payload.custPoNumber,
               "deliveryDate": payload.deliveryDate,
               "deliveryTime": payload.deliveryTime,
               "locationText": payload.locationText,
               "notes": payload.notes,
               "plantId": payload.plantId,
               "plantName": payload.plantName,
               "products": payload.products,
               "services": payload.services,
               "shipToId": payload.shipToId,
               "shipToName1": payload.shipToName1,
               "shipToName2": payload.shipToName2,
               "soldToId": payload.soldToId,
               "soldToName1": payload.soldToName1,
               "soldToName2": payload.soldToName2,
               "shotId": payload.shotId,
               "vehicleOperators": payload.vehicleOperators
            }
         }
      }
   }
}

Besides that, you are mixing async/await and Promises for asynchronous execution. Is your JS Rule markes as "async"?

Best regards,

Robin

entry.title: AMS: can authorization policies be combined/shared across multiple CAP apps?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm exploring how AMS handles authorization across several independent CAP apps. Each app has its own IAS application and deploys its own DCL base policies. My goal is to understand whether one assignment can cover policies from multiple apps.

What I tried:

  1. Reuse service – From the documentation I couldn't work out how a CAP app is actually linked to a reuse service. Setting consumed-services on the consuming app's IAS instance had no effect: the reuse app didn't appear under "Reuse services consumed by this application", and no reuse dropdown showed in the consuming app's Authorization Policies tab.
  2. Combine Authorization Policies (Add Combination) – Only policies from the same application are selectable.

The docs say a combined policy can "bundle authorization policies within or across solutions":

Questions:

  • Is combining/sharing policies across applications supported for custom CAP apps, or only for SAP-delivered bundled solutions?
  • If supported, what establishes the relationship so another app's base policies become selectable?
  • If not, is the intended pattern to centralize assignment via Identity Directory groups (SCIM2/IPS)?

entry.title: Unable to Enable Open Item Management for Existing G/L Accounts with Postings in SAP S/4HANA Public
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are facing an issue while trying to enable Open Item Management for 3 existing G/L accounts in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

These G/L accounts are currently being used as Nostro Bank G/L accounts and already contain posted transactions in the production system. Based on a new business requirement, we need to enable the Open Item Management indicator for these G/L accounts.

However, when we attempt to activate the Open Item Management indicator, the system does not allow the change because the G/L accounts already have posted transactions in the production system.

Proposed Approach:

One possible solution we are considering is as follows:

  1. Transfer all existing postings from the current G/L account to another (temporary or new) G/L account.
  2. Once all postings are moved and the original G/L account no longer contains any transactions, enable the Open Item Management indicator on the original G/L account.
  3. Continue using the original G/L account as an open item managed account going forward.

However, we are unsure whether this approach is supported or recommended in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Could you please advise on the following?

  1. Is the above approach supported by SAP?
  2. Is there any standard SAP-supported procedure to enable Open Item Management for existing G/L accounts with posted transactions?
  3. If the proposed approach is not recommended, what is the SAP best practice for handling this scenario in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  4. Are there any alternative solutions that would allow us to enable Open Item Management without creating new G/L accounts?

We would appreciate any guidance or recommendations from the community.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Thanks & regards,

Jatin Mehrani

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Dataaction resultlookup
entry.content:
@MK70 

Nested IF /ELSEIF  keeps narrowing the scope with each IF statement. Inner IF is restricted to the scope of its outer IF statement. Just explore IF with AND condition rather nested IF.

IF resultlookup() AND resultlookup () THEN // to combine multiple conditions.

ENDIF

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Default Counted Date Based on Physical Inventory Coun...
entry.content:
Dear @S0027269767,

No. There is currently no standard configuration / SSCUI or released in-app extensibility option to automatically default the Count Date of Uncounted Items to the Planned Count Date or to the date when Book Inventory Freeze was set

In the standard Manage Physical Inventory Count app, SAP confirms that the field Count Date of Uncounted Items defaults to today date. SAP documented resolution is that the user opens the General Information tab and manually changes the date before saving the count. This matches the described scenario where the physical inventory document was created and frozen on 27.06.2026, but the count is entered later, for example on 30.06.2026

More details below:

SAP KBA 3614732 – Unable to Post Physical Inventory Count in Previous Posting Period Due to Error M7053

SAP KBA 3633188 – Create / Enter a Past Count Date in Physical Inventory Document

The Book Inventory Freeze indicator does not control this date defaulting. Its purpose is to freeze the book inventory balance in the physical inventory document so that later goods movements do not change the book quantity relevant for the inventory difference calculation. It does not automatically derive or overwrite the count entry date

If automation is required the clean-core option would be an integration-based count-entry process outside the standard Fiori app, using the released Physical Inventory Document API / integration option and passing the count date explicitly. SAP documents the count operation for physical inventory document items and also confirms that multiple count PATCH operations in one change set must use the same counting date

More details below:

SAP Help – Count Physical Inventory Document Items

SAP Help – Operations for Physical Inventory Document API

IMPORTANT: Where the standard functionality does not meet the business requirement the correct approach is to submit an improvement request in SAP Customer Influence. This is the SAP-recommended channel for non-go-live-critical enhancement requests where customers and partners can submit and vote for product improvements

More details below:

SAP KBA - 2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

To avoid the validation in late save phase, you need to use the PRECHECK.

PRECHECK can be implemented for CRUD operation and Actions as well.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Bring data from a parent accoun...
entry.content:
Hola @BeatrizDuenas ,

I dug into both scripts and found the issue.

Your hardcoded version works because the BASEMEMBER in the IF condition resolves to the real leaf children of 3015855 (3409370 and 3828303), and one of them has booked data. RESULTLOOKUP is reading that leaf, not the parent's rollup.

The dynamic version has a straightforward bug: DATA() still hardcodes the write target to 3015880_REF_2424040, so even though your IF now dynamically matches both 3015880 and 3015897, every result gets forced into that one account.

Before fixing that, check one thing: the Aggregation Type on account 3015855.

  • If it's SUM, RESULTLOOKUP should be able to read the parent's rolled-up value directly once CONFIG.READ_CALCULATED_MEMBER_VALUES is on, no staging needed. In that case, just drop the hardcoded account from DATA() and move DELETE() inside the IF so it clears whatever account is currently in scope, not always 3015880.
  • If it's NONE, that's the real blocker. A NONE-aggregation parent doesn't collapse to one number when its children differ, so RESULTLOOKUP has nothing consistent to return.

If the above fix does not work, this other workaround definitely will work as I had to do the same for a client where "CONFIG.READ_CALCULATED_MEMBER_VALUES " was not available: stage the parent's value into a real leaf account first, then point ID_PL_REPETIDO at that leaf instead of at the parent.

  1. Create a new leaf account (e.g. 3015855_AUX) under the same hierarchy branch.
  2. Add a step before your current one to populate it with the parent data:
 
DATA([d/Account_PyG_Balance] = "3015855_AUX") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Account_PyG_Balance] = BASEMEMBER([d/Account_PyG_Balance].[h/PARENTH02], "3015855_REF_2424040"))
  1. Update ID_PL_REPETIDO on 3015880 and 3015897 to point to "3015855_AUX" instead of "3015855_REF_2424040".
  2. Keep the dynamic script as-is, it now reads a real leaf, so there's no ambiguity.

Let me know what worked in your case in the end.

entry.title: Incoming Bank Statement Clearing for Batch Payments Without Enabling Extended Individual Payments
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are currently processing supplier payments through Automatic Payments (APP), where multiple payments are bundled into a single batch and transmitted to the bank as a consolidated payment file. As a result, the bank statement is also received as a single batch entry rather than individual payment transactions.

Our requirement is to automatically clear the corresponding open items when processing the incoming bank statement, without enabling "Extended Individual Payment" or maintaining settings at the vendor master data level, as these options would result in additional bank charges.

We have heard from another SAP customer that, even when payments are sent in batch format, a specific reference value is populated during the automatic payment run. This reference is then included in the bank statement and is used by the system to identify and clear the related open items automatically.

In our case, however, the reference field is coming through as blank, which prevents automatic clearing.

Could anyone please clarify:

  • Is there any standard SAP configuration that populates a reference for batch payments generated through APP?
  • Can this reference be transferred to the payment file and subsequently returned in the incoming bank statement?
  • Is there any alternative approach to achieve automatic clearing of batch payments without enabling Extended Individual Payment?
  • Which configuration settings, payment medium parameters, or interpretation algorithms should be checked to support this functionality?

For reference, I have attached a screenshot showing the field that remains blank in our system.

Any guidance or experience with similar scenarios would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

Regards

Naveen Kumar

entry.title: Re: Invoice reference/ Payment reference from contract
entry.content:
Hello!

Recommended field for invoice-to-payment matching is Payment Reference

Invoice Reference is not relevant to this use case. Is an internal FI linkage field, blank on original invoices; only used on follow-up documents (credit memos/payments).

Payment Reference is not automatically generated for the rent invoices created via RE-FX "Create Invoice" because payment reference generation is not activated for this posting flow.

  • If classic FI-AR: Payment Reference generation is controlled at the document type/output level (structured reference number generation), not the FI-CA "Payment Reference Model" mechanism referenced earlier.
  • If FI-CA: the FI-CA configuration path is the right direction.

Activate Payment Reference generation for the confirmed engine and company code/country.

Verify the field is populated on the resulting FI document, check via Manage Customer Line Items or Display Line Items in General Ledger that Payment Reference is no longer blank.

Locate the field in the form template data source RE_CN_INVOICE_DE:

If output form in use  is Adobe-based: open the predelivered template in Maintain Form Templates and inspect the Data View for the Payment Reference node.

If the field is not exposed in the standard data source, extend it to custom field enablement for the form template plus a CDS extension to populate it..

entry.title: Re: Warm welcome from a UX strategist
entry.content:
Of course! For me, decision confidence is a key measure of success and ROI. I'm less interested in the AI capability itself, and more concerned in if a user a) feels confident in trusting the AIs content, and b) how they act in response. 

One of the common challenges i'm observing is around trust. There's a spectrum of users either trusting the AI answers too much and no longer applying any critical thinking, which becomes a business risk in certain contexts, to users at the other end who are approaching it with a mindset of distrust and sticking with manual or outdated processes. Then there's the goldilocks zone in the middle where I'm seeing users highly engaged with AI and are operating with enough domain knowledge to ask the right questions, they're treating AI as a decision-supporting tool rather than a blind decision-making one. 

There's a lot of opportunity in this space to add real value to both the business and our users, especially with reducing cognitive load, repetitive tasks, and the need to work across fragmented internal systems just to access information faster. The key is that the users remain accountable and have enough context to validate any AI recommendations before acting on them.

It might also be worth saying that i'm looking beyond quantitative metrics like adoption and utilisation to determine success, and primarily focusing on qualitative insights. Saying someone engaged with an AI agent 100 times doesn’t show if it delivered value, but saying they were able to analyse data faster or complete tasks in fewer steps is gold.

entry.title: Digital Payment Addon V2- Stripe V2 PSP- External Token & reauthorisation -Use Case
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am working on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation and need guidance on the recommended payment design for web portal sales orders using SAP Digital Payments add-on with Stripe as the payment service provider.

Main question: Can SAP Digital Payments with the Stripe V2 adapter support a flow where an externally created Stripe payment method token is registered through Payment Card Preparation, stored as an SAP Digital Payments card reference, and later used by SAP to create authorizations and captures for partial deliveries?

Scenario overview

Current process: MarketTime, the web portal, generates a file containing sales order details and the payment card token for each order. After the order is created in SAP Business One, the credit team uses the token from the MarketTime file to capture payment when the order is shipped. This can involve multiple deliveries against the same sales order. The current payment service provider is Authorize.net.

Proposed target process: Stripe will replace Authorize.net as the payment service provider, and SAP Digital Payments will be used for payment card registration, authorization, settlement, and reconciliation.

  1. SAP CPI will process the file to create Sales order in S4 Public cloud.
  2. SAP CPI will register the Stripe payment method token in SAP Digital Payments using the Payment Card Preparation process. The token will originate from Stripe and will be provided in the MarketTime file before the SAP sales order is created.
  3. The SAP Sales Order will store the resulting SAP Digital Payment card reference.
  4. Authorization will be performed later from SAP when the order is ready for fulfilment.
  5. Settlement and reconciliation will be performed using standard SAP Digital Payments processes.

We would appreciate confirmation on the following points:

  1. External Token Registration and Authorization

Can SAP Digital Payments (Stripe V2 adapter) create a payment authorization using a payment method token that originated outside SAP and was subsequently registered in SAP Digital Payments using the Payment Card Preparation process?

Proposed flow:

MarketTime → Stripe Token Creation → Payment Card Preparation → SAP DPA Card Reference → Authorization from SAP → Settlement

 

  1. Authorization Creation Using Stored Card Token

After a payment card has been registered in SAP Digital Payments via Payment Card Preparation, can SAP create a new payment authorization against that stored card token using the Stripe V2 adapter?

 

  1. Expired Authorization Handling

If an authorization created through SAP Digital Payments subsequently expires before settlement:

  • Does the Stripe V2 adapter support reauthorization of the existing authorization?
    • If reauthorization is not supported, can SAP Digital Payments create a completely new authorization using the stored payment card token?
  1. Partial Shipment / Backorder Scenarios

For orders that ship in multiple deliveries:

  • Can SAP perform multiple captures against a single authorization (multicapture)?
    • If the original authorization expires before all deliveries are shipped, can SAP create a replacement authorization using the stored payment card token?
    • Are there any Stripe V2 limitations for partial shipment or backorder scenarios?
  1. Strong Customer Authentication (SCA)

Are there any Stripe V2 limitations related to Strong Customer Authentication when:

  • Creating a new authorization from a previously registered card token?
    • Creating a replacement authorization after an authorization has expired?
  1. Recommended SAP Design

For the proposed MarketTime scenario, would SAP recommend:

  1. Payment Card Preparation → Authorization from SAP → Settlement from SAP

or

  1. MarketTime / Stripe authorization externally → Authorization Preparation → Settlement from SAP

We are trying to determine the most supportable approach for MarketTime orders while minimizing manual payment processing and avoiding issues caused by authorization expiry.

Thanks,

Sagar

entry.title: Re: SAC Excel Add-in - Unable to Maintain Cost Center & G...
entry.content:
@adelpriore26 

SAC Excel Add-in Filter support is basic and mostly on member selection or exclude. If your story had advanced filtering /complex filters, those might be dropped as there is no support for that in SAC Excel Add-in. Depending on your use case you could try the use of SAP.OVERWRITEDIMENSIONFILTER function to control the filters to some extent.

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Exploring Application Frontend Service: Deploying the UI of an MTA App
entry.content:
Hi Mio

Thanks for the blog. Some updates for reference 

  1. The workaround you described above to generate the deployment configuration for the HTML5 Repo and then change them to AFS is not needed. The CDS CLI now supports 'cds add app-front' which adds corresponding entry to the package.json file and later when you 'cds add mta' will add the needed setup for AFS in the mta.yaml file
  2.  The issue with redeploying application with afctl that overwrite the app configs is fixed. If no config.json is provided in the deployment, all configurations will be taken from the previous deployment

entry.title: Re: German B2B E-Invoicing Mandate - Current State for SA...
entry.content:
Hello Mike,

In general, for E-Invoicing - here are the approaches that we have considered
1) Via Peppol - using  SAP DRC ( Document and reporting compliance) -- this is a additional Licences and subscription that is required to be set up on SAP BTP. withing BTP, enable DRC. and Once DRC is set up, activate peppol exchange service.
This is supported as standard / suggested option. this will be automated approach meaning invoice created in ByDesign is then automatically created on DRC / peppol and sent to tax authorities.

Check : Integration with Peppol Exchange | SAP Help Portal and Integrating the Cloud Edition with SAP Business ByDesign | SAP Help Portal



2) XSLT -
Blogs you mentioned, Provides the set up steps and template that you can use.  This template XSLT files are good starting point to customize further for the specific needs.
We have few customers who are using XSLT transformations for the German E-invoicing mandate. however we have used the template and customized them for the customer needs and created a custom XSLT files that can be used for the German E-invoicing.

This approach is manual. meaning when invoice is created, system generates XML file that can be manually downloaded and imported to tax authority. ( and if needed, Along side the XML, you can also generate standard PDF as well).


looking at overall stretegy, being SAP Bydesign is in maintenance mode,  XSLT ( low volume and manual approach is ok with manual maintenance when rules or structure changes for e-invoicing), DRC/ Peppol ( high volume and automated approach) or third part add on ( Some external third party tool supports German and other countries too, and if there is need for e-invoicing for other countries - it could be viable option)

Let me know if you need further details

Thanks,
Harshal

entry.title: Announcing iWorkshop - A SAP Community Meetup Focused on SAP BTP Integration Suite
entry.content:
The SAP Integration landscape is evolving faster than ever. Modern enterprises are no longer integrating only SAP systems they're connecting SAP with CRM platforms, HR solutions, e-commerce applications, collaboration tools, AI services, cloud platforms, and countless third-party applications.

Choosing the right integration approach, selecting the appropriate adapter, and designing scalable connectivity patterns have become essential skills for every integration professional.

With that in mind, we're excited to announce iWorkshop – A Practitioner Session on SAP 3rd Party Connectivity, an interactive SAP Community event dedicated to SAP BTP Integration Suite.

Unlike traditional technical sessions, iWorkshop is designed around one simple idea:

The best way to learn integration is by experiencing it.

What makes iWorkshop different?

This isn't a conference where you spend hours watching presentations.

It's an interactive, community-driven experience where participants will learn from real-world scenarios, collaborate with fellow professionals, and put their integration knowledge to the test.

Practitioner Sessions

Hear directly from integration practitioners as they share real customer experiences, best practices, and practical guidance on building third-party integrations using SAP BTP Integration Suite.

Topics include:

  • Third-Party Connectivity with SAP Integration Suite

  • HTTP Adapter vs. Application Adapters – Choosing the right adapter for the right scenario

  • Practical architecture considerations and implementation tips

The Adapter Files – Scavenger Hunt

Learning doesn't have to stop at slides.

Our signature workshop activity, The Adapter Files, transforms learning into an interactive team challenge.

Participants will work together to solve clues, identify the right adapters, and tackle integration scenarios inspired by real-world connectivity challenges. Along the way, you'll sharpen your problem-solving skills, exchange ideas with teammates, and enjoy some friendly competition.

And yes... the best adapter architect in the room won't leave empty-handed!

Connect with the SAP Integration Community

One of the greatest strengths of the SAP Community is the people behind it.

Whether you're an experienced integration architect or just starting your journey with SAP BTP Integration Suite, iWorkshop offers a fantastic opportunity to connect with developers, architects, consultants, and integration enthusiasts who share the same passion for enterprise integration.

Every conversation is an opportunity to learn something new.

Event Details

Date: 8 August 2026

Time: 8:30 AM – 1:30 PM

Venue: Rojo Integrations, WeWork, Eleven West, Pancard Road, Baner, Pune

Registration: https://www.eventbrite.nl/e/iworkshop-by-rojo-integrations-tickets-1992624446359?aff=oddtdtcreator 

If you're passionate about SAP Integration Suite, curious about third-party connectivity, or simply looking to engage with the SAP Community in Pune, we'd love to have you join us.

Come for the technology.

Stay for the conversations.

Leave with new ideas, new connections, and a fresh perspective on SAP Integration.

We look forward to welcoming you to iWorkshop. See you in Pune!

 

Regards,

SAP Community Pune

entry.content:
Hello Max,

well it depends. Is document splitting for the G/L already active in the system in the first place? If not, then you would need to implement a separate implementation project on the subsequent introduction of document splitting, first. There is no shortcut to this.

If document splitting has already been enabled in Accounting, you should still be able to edit the controlling related additional split criteria, even if the system is already productive.

Of course this is subject to your organisation's testing and acceptance criteria. I would suggest to test it out in recent copy of the production system.

KR,

Steffen

entry.title: Re: PO number should be available in the payment proposal...
entry.content:
Dear @S002476221520,

The PO number cannot be added at header level during payment proposal creation in the standard Manage Automatic Payments app. This is standard system design, not a missing authorization or personalization issue. For analysis or traceability the PO reference should be checked from the underlying supplier invoice / related documents instead of from the payment proposal header:

  • Use Manage Journal Entries and open Related Documents to see connected purchasing/invoice documents
  • Use Display Process Flow – Accounts Payable to view the document flow
  • For reporting/extraction use the CDS views I_SUPPLIERINVOICEAPI01 and I_SUPLRINVCITEMPURORDREFAPI01 to derive the supplier invoice and related PO reference. SAP lists these alternatives in the KBA

More details below:

SAP KBA 3772482 - Purchasing Document and Purchasing Document Item not displayed in Manage Supplier Line Items app - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

IMPORTANT: Where the standard functionality does not meet the business requirement the correct approach is to submit an improvement request in SAP Customer Influence. This is the SAP-recommended channel for non-go-live-critical enhancement requests where customers and partners can submit and vote for product improvements

More details below:

SAP KBA 2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
entry.content:
@LauraHRC Hello -- I received this some time ago from an SAP Consultant in response the same question I had about Onboardees that were already in flight.  We have not made the switch yet because it scares me and I don't know what is going to break but will be forced to make the switch soon.

I asked your question about the inflight onboardees impact when enabling IAS and below what I found. Basically it says If they should be able to continue to use their original login method and it’s triggered based on needing access to PDC (Personal Data Collection). This sounds like if they have passed PDC step, then they will use their original login method. If they haven’t got PDC yet, then they will trigger the IAS creation.

Impact on Inflight Onboarding External Users When IAS is Enabled

When IAS is enabled for onboarding external users, the impact on inflight (already in-progress) onboarding processes depends on several factors:

Immediate Impact on Existing Onboardees

 For users already in the onboarding process:

  • Existing onboardees who haven't completed their process will continue using the original authentication method until their status changes
  • The extension status determines sync behavior: 0 (Active) syncs to IAS, 1 (Inactive) doesn't sync, and 2 (Pending) prevents sync even for active users
  • Users with "Pending" status won't be synced to IAS until their input is required (typically when PDC is needed)

Authentication Method Changes

 Before IAS Integration:

  • Onboardees received password reset links to set passwords in SuccessFactors HCM Suite
  • Login URLs included the pm_product_name=ONB parameter
  • Authentication was handled directly by SuccessFactors

After IAS Integration:

  • New onboardees are synced to IAS in real-time after completing the New Hire Data Review step
  • They receive IAS Account Activation emails to set passwords in IAS
  • Login URLs no longer include the pm_product_name=ONB parameter
  • Authentication is handled through IAS

Email Communication Impact

 Dual Email Risk:

  • If welcome email templates aren't disabled in HCM Suite, onboardees may receive two welcome emails - one from HCM Suite and one from IAS
  • It's recommended to disable the following templates in Email Services:
    • Template: (ONB) External User Welcome Message Template
    • Template: (ONB) Rehire User Welcome Message Template

Transition Recommendations

  1. Enable real-time sync to ensure immediate account provisioning and deprovisioning
  2. Update email templates to remove password reset links and include IAS activation instructions
  3. Configure proper home URL redirects for post-activation navigation
  4. Monitor the sync status through Admin Center execution dashboards

Sources:

https://me.sap.com/notes/3204536 - 3204536 - How to Setup up Identity Authentication Service (IAS) for Onboarding External Users – Onboarding

https://me.sap.com/notes/3281873 - 3281873 - [Onboarding] IAS Main KBA

Onboarding New Hires Authentication using SAP Iden... - SAP Community
